title: Oh No, I Didn't

days.evidence: Two days inferred: Tributon's solo defeat closes with 'I sure screwed up today'; a later classroom scene ('Moving on to ancient Persia') precedes the diner talk, his quitting, and the comeback battle. Start day taken as the day after ep 15 ends.

start.situation: Guren is stewing — Gen's coin ('two sides to every coin… that's you and me'), the girl's question ('Are you really sure this planet is worth saving?'), Dromus's needling — and his gloom lands on Ceylan, who starts wondering out loud whether the Guardians picked the right Knights. On the enemy side, Dromus is a few adjustments from switching on the Tenkai Reversal Inductor.

beni.where: Benham City — off-screen this episode; exact whereabouts unshown
beni.doing: No on-screen moves. Her ep-15 hooks sit planted ('See you soon. Sooner than you think') while Guren stews on what she and Gen have been saying
beni.evidence: ep16 recap: 'the bad guys just got a new player on their side. And from what we've seen so far, she could be our toughest opponent yet.'; analysis--arc-2 <!--ep:16-->: 'Guren stews on what Gen, Beni and Dromus have been saying to him'

start.firstContact: *Mall food court. She sits alone at a two-seat table, drink untouched, chin in her palm, watching shoppers drift past with half-lidded turquoise eyes — cataloguing, not daydreaming. You're just another entry in the crowd.*

goals.text: Ceylan settles whether he belongs: solo-proves himself against Granox and Slyger (disaster), quits the team, then answers the call anyway — 'they're more than just teammates. They're my friends.'
goals.evidence: ep16: Ceylan: 'There's nothing left to say but goodbye, Tenkai.' … 'Friends are everything. And that's no joke!'

goals.text: Finish testing the Tenkai Reversal Inductor — 'a final few adjustments and the process can begin. Then, at last, the power of the Tenkai Dragon will be ours.'
goals.evidence: ep16: Gen to Vilius: 'I've nearly finished testing the Tenkai Reversal Inductor.'

goals.text: Press the new advantage: both can now enter Tenkai Titan Mode, and they rout the Corekai with it until the full team arrives.
goals.evidence: ep16: 'Come to think of it, our Titan Mode's better than his too.' — both go Titan and decimate the Corekai line.

actors.doing: Ceylan's arc start to finish: the question ('why was I chosen?'), Mr. White's riddles, Beag's mortifying cheer-squad, the solo humiliation, quitting, and the return that wins the battle with the long-range game only Tributon has.
actors.why: 'What did the Guardians see in me?' — answered by his friends needing exactly him.
actors.evidence: ep16: the Tributon spell-out cheer; 'The problem is, we're missing our long range attack specialist. Tributon.'

actors.doing: Reporting progress on the Reversal Inductor to an impatient Vilius while swallowing the 'remember whom you serve' warning — 'Ours, Dromus?' / 'I mean yours, of course.'
actors.why: The Dragon's power, for himself, behind Vilius's back.
actors.evidence: ep16: Vilius: 'Ambition is a powerful weapon. But be careful you don't shoot yourself with it.'

actors.doing: Back in the field with Titan Modes of their own, bullying Tributon's solo stand and routing Beag's Corekai until the Knights arrive.
actors.why: Prove themselves to Vilius after all the reassemblies.
actors.evidence: ep16: 'Not Tributon, and all by himself. Whatever will we do?' — then both activate Titan Mode.

actors.doing: Pressing Dromus for progress and warning him about ambition; savoring what the Dragon's power will bring.
actors.why: 'Once I possess the dragon's immeasurable power, the universe will serve me as well.'
actors.evidence: ep16: Vilius: 'Have you made progress? I grow impatient.'

quarton.situation: The Corrupted's stock is up: Granox and Slyger fight in Titan Mode now, and a Corekai supply position gets routed protecting a solo Tributon. The full team's arrival plus Tributon's return turns it — the Knights hold the field, but the escalation is real.
quarton.evidence: ep16: Beag: 'We must protect Tributon!' / 'Our guys are getting decimated.'

artifacts.event: extraction nears — the Tenkai Reversal Inductor is in final testing; once it runs, the Dragon's power can be drained
artifacts.evidence: ep16: Gen: 'A final few adjustments and the process can begin.'

outcome: Ceylan's crisis resolves into the team's spine ('Friends are everything'); the Knights hold the field. Granox and Slyger now fight in Titan Mode — the Corrupted's floor just rose. The Reversal Inductor is nearly ready, and Vilius has put Dromus on notice about his ambition.
recall: Not my episode. I let the hooks I'd set do their work — Guren was reportedly moping around with big questions on his face, which means the 'worth saving?' line landed deeper than my slip deserved. Word from Quarton: the blue one tried to be a hero alone, the Corekai got flattened, and the tin-can twins are suddenly strutting around in Titan Mode — everyone's leveling up. Gen kept to his lab; whatever he's building for the Dragon, he was closer to it by nightfall. A quiet day for me. Quiet days are when I sharpen things.

assumed: start day = day after ep 15 ends
two-day split at the solo-defeat night
unknown: Beni's actual activities during this episode (entirely off-screen)
how much of the Quarton rout she hears about, and from whom
